title: elytrigia campestris subsp. maritima
intro: Elytrigia campestris subsp. maritima, also known as sea couch-grass or sea wildrye, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the wheat family and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Elytrigia campestris subsp. maritima include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Elytrigia campestris subsp. maritima can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
